title: lygodium forsteri
intro: Lygodium forsteri, also known as the forster's fern or forster's climbing fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lygodiaceae. It is a type of climbing fern native to Australia and New Zealand. The leaves of Lygodium forsteri have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Lygodium forsteri, such as teas and infusions, are said to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
